titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5957

p.seg: Liulf son of Ælsi and his heirs, namely, Cospatric, Gamel, Macbeth, Reginald, and Ecgheard, to all who view or hear these letters send greeting.

in the court of the prior of Coldingham, and in his presence, to have abjured whatever right we have had in Little Reston and to have quitclaimed it to Adam son of Cospatric and his heirs, in respect of ourselves or our successors, without any trouble or counter-claim, in return for seven merks and one horse of two merks which he has given to us in the presence of many worthy men. And since we will that it should be firm, we have affirmed by faith the oath to be sworn, in the hands of Walter Oliphant, the king's justice, to be held for ever without any deceit or counter-claim.

With these witnesses: Walter Oliphant, the king's justice; Robert of Burneville, sheriff, and his heir, Robert; Richard the marischal; Patrick, the earl's uncle; Adam son of Aldan, and his heir, Patrick; Gilbert Fraser, and John and Bernard, his sons; Richard de Merlay; Stephen Papedy; Waldef of Prenderguest; Edward of Oldcambus and his sons, Thomas, William, Stephen; William the grieve; William of Nisbet; Adam son of Pace; Adam of Prenderguest; Elias of Ayton; Adam of Lamberton; Alan of Swinton; Richard of Swinton; Richard of Lumsdaine; Uchtred son of Colman, and Adam his son; Waldef son of Uchtred; Ralph son of Godwin; Adam of Beoch; Thomas son of Siward, and his heir, Patrick; Adam son of Leysing; Eilaf son of Oddger, and his heir; Robert the cook, and Philip of the cellar; and many others.
